candidateContexts.label: admin basic-info authority boundary: JWT filter, ADMIN role gate, permitAll read routes, open CORS
admin basic-info price CRUD surface fronting ts-price-service, with rate representation as double
admin order CRUD proxy surface routing between ts-order-service and ts-order-other-service by trainNumber prefix
admin order authority boundary: JWT filter, ADMIN role gate on whole adminorderservice path
admin route CRUD surface with station-existence precondition before route creation
admin route authority boundary: JWT filter, ADMIN role gate on whole adminrouteservice path

candidateCovers.label: authority boundary measurement surface across ts-admin-basic-info-service, ts-admin-order-service, ts-admin-route-service
admin facade proxy pattern fronting downstream raw services (basic-info/order/route)

notes: Spring Boot bootstrap class, registers a load-balanced RestTemplate bean and enables discovery client; no domain logic.
Five GET read routes are permitAll while the rest of the adminbasicservice path requires ADMIN role; open CORS bean allows any origin/method/header with credentials disabled.
21 endpoints across 5 CRUD resource groups (contacts/stations/trains/configs/prices); every endpoint except /welcome carries an explicit @CrossOrigin(origins="*") annotation.
Price rate fields are plain double primitives, no BigDecimal or string parsing observed at this site.
Interface declares 20 CRUD methods all returning the shared Response type; the implementing class carries the capability atoms.
Pure passthrough proxy: every method forwards to exactly one downstream service and returns re.getBody() with no status inspection, unlike the order and route service implementations.
Declares service name ts-admin-basic-info-service, port 18767, and nacos discovery server address list.
Unlike the basic-info service, the whole adminorderservice path requires ADMIN role with no permitAll read routes.
Only the GET /adminorder endpoint carries @CrossOrigin; the add/update/delete endpoints do not, unlike the basic-info controller where every endpoint carries it.
getAllOrders aggregates unconditionally from both ts-order-service and ts-order-other-service; the other three methods branch on trainNumber prefix to pick exactly one of the two providers.
Declares service name ts-admin-order-service, port 16112, nacos discovery server address list.
Same shape as the order service SecurityConfig: whole adminrouteservice path requires ADMIN role, no permitAll read routes.
Same asymmetric CORS pattern as the order controller: only the GET list endpoint carries @CrossOrigin.

createAndModifyRoute validates start/end stations against the requested station list before calling checkStationsExists, which itself calls ts-station-service and interprets a null map value as a not-found station.
Declares service name ts-admin-route-service, port 16113, nacos discovery server address list.
Bootstrap class only; controller/service/security files for this service are outside this chunk.
